feed.title: Segwit commitment to post-quantum witness data?

entry.title: Segwit commitment to post-quantum witness data?
entry.summary:
The email discusses a technical enhancement in blockchain technology, likening the introduction of each new "style" to a combination of soft-fork, block storage, and peer-to-peer (p2p) upgrade, similar to the implementation of Segregated Witness (SegWit). The advantage highlighted is that once the initial logic for a new style is established, enabling subsequent styles primarily involves defining a new weighting rule and determining an activation trigger. This suggests a streamlined process compared to previous implementations.
Furthermore, for nodes that do not immediately upgrade to incorporate the new logic, there is a requirement to re-download blocks post-activation to access the additional data. This could lead to situations where blocks that were considered valid before the activation might be invalidated if they fail to align with the new style data. Despite the theoretical complexities akin to those experienced during the deployment of SegWit, the process is anticipated to be more straightforward in practice due to the repetitive application of the same underlying logic. This iterative approach implies a reduction in complexity with each new style introduced, potentially simplifying the integration and adoption process across the network.
